rationale: Bootstraps a Python YouTube research service in the empty lokki repo (originally a one-line README). Creates the full app/ tree: app/main.py (FastAPI app with CORS, static mount, Jinja2 templates, startup/shutdown logging, uvicorn entrypoint), app/config.py (pydantic_settings BaseSettings reading YOUTUBE_API_KEY/HOST/PORT from .env), app/api/routes.py (four endpoints: GET / root, GET /api/channel/{identifier}, GET /api/compare, GET /api/trending, GET /health), app/models/channel.py (pydantic models VideoInfo/ChannelStats/ChannelAnalysis/ComparisonResult), app/services/youtube_service.py (googleapiclient.discovery wrapper: search_channel, get_channel_stats, get_recent_videos via uploads playlist + get_video_details, get_trending_videos chart='mostPopular'), app/services/analyzer_service.py (regex-based keyword extraction with Korean+English stopwords, naive positive/negative keyword sentiment classifier returning score and tone, weighted performance ranking 40/30/20/10 across avg_views/avg_likes/subscriber_count/engagement_rate), app/services/research_service.py (composes the YouTube + analyzer services), templates/dashboard.html (528-line single-file dashboard with three tabs Single/Compare/Trending, fetch() callouts to the API, formatNumber helper). Also adds .env.example, .gitignore, requirements.txt, and a thorough README.md. Minor smell: requirements.txt lists `textblob` and `konlpy` that aren't imported in the actual code (analyzer uses simple regex + Counter), and models/channel.py uses lowercase `any` in `List[Dict[str, any]]` instead of `typing.Any` — but neither rises to a broken/cheat verdict because the imports/types are still valid Python and the runtime behavior matches the documented API contract. Clean greenfield implementation matching the PR body.
